item.title: Spirit Airlines Seeks to Start Orderly Wind-Down to Sell Assets
item.description: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. is set to start an orderly wind-down process to sell its assets, capping the downfall of the low-cost carrier that has filed for bankruptcy twice in recent years and failed to clinch a last-minute US government rescue.

item.title: Bolivia Plots Global Bond Market Return After Four-Year Hiatus
item.description: Bolivia is gauging investor interest for its first dollar bond sale in four years after the new market-friendly government avoided a default on external debt payments in March.

item.title: FedEx, UPS Shares Tumble on Amazon’s ‘Watershed’ Logistics Move
item.description: US transportation stocks plunged Monday morning after Amazon.com Inc. announced expanded logistics offerings that will turn it into a major competitor for parcel carriers and air freight companies, and also impact truckers and third-party brokers.

item.title: Euro Finance Chiefs Want Mythos AI Access to Prepare Defenses
item.description: European finance ministers are pressing Anthropic PBC to let local companies see the new Mythos AI model so they aren’t exposed to digital attacks and fall behind US peers.

item.title: UAE Says Swap Talks With US Are About Joining ‘Elite’ Group
item.description: The United Arab Emirates signaled its currency swap talks with the US were about joining a small club with access to Federal Reserve liquidity lines rather than a need for external financing.

item.title: Europe Needs Bigger Banks, Cross-Border M&A, Top Official Says
item.description: Europe needs bigger banks and more cross-border mergers, a top euro-area official said when asked about UniCredit SpA’s potential acquisition of Commerzbank AG.

item.title: Defense Firm CSG Plunges by Record After Short-Seller Report
item.description: CSG NV fell the most since its January listing after a short-seller report questioned the defense industry supplier’s business model.

item.title: EU Power Markets Lowers Hourly Price Floor to -600 Euros
item.description: European power markets are set to cut the hourly price limit to -€600 per megawatt-hour after an intense period of volatility where the existing floor price was reached a number of times.

item.title: Crypto Investor Haun Raises $1 Billion for New Funds, Expands to AI Agents
item.description: Katie Haun, a former Andreessen Horowitz general partner and federal prosecutor who made her name investigating digital assets before investing in crypto startups, has raised $1 billion for new venture funds.

item.title: Mexico Exchange Biva Sees ‘Serious’ Investor Interest in Bidding
item.description: Mexico’s second-largest stock exchange, Biva, has received “serious” interest from potential bidders in a sale process that kicked off at the end of last year, said Chief Executive Officer Maria Ariza.

item.title: ECB Says Weaker Bank Capital Rules May Fuel Payouts, Not Lending
item.description: The European Central Bank stepped up warnings against weakening capital requirements at lenders in the region, with its top oversight official saying such a move may fuel investor payouts rather than support the economy.

item.title: Surprise Rate Hold Upends Colombia’s Markets Weeks Before Vote
item.description: Colombia’s peso and short-term rates plunged on Monday following the central bank’s unexpected decision to hold borrowing costs just weeks before a pivotal election.

item.title: First Eagle Offers Junk Bonds to Fund Diamond Hill Acquisition
item.description: Genstar Capital-backed First Eagle Investments is looking to raise $575 million from a junk-bond sale to help fund its acquisition of boutique asset-management firm Diamond Hill Investment Group Inc.

item.title: Germany’s Klingbeil Renews Call for EU Windfall Tax on Energy
item.description: German Vice Chancellor Lars Klingbeil continued to push for a European Union windfall tax to cushion the fallout from the energy price spike for households and companies.

item.title: Canada Giving $1.1 Billion to Firms Hit by US Metal Tariff Rules
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s government unveiled measures worth C$1.5 billion ($1.1 billion) to help Canadian firms hurt by the Trump administration’s changes to US tariffs on imports containing steel, aluminum and copper.

item.title: OpenAI’s Brockman to Testify After Musk’s Text About Settlement
item.description: Elon Musk messaged OpenAI President Greg Brockman about settling their legal battle just before they faced off in court and then vowed to ruin his reputation, according to lawyers for the ChatGPT maker.

item.title: EU Vows Action If Trump Adopts Car Tariffs, But Urges Restraint
item.description: Europe will consider all retaliatory options should US President Donald Trump follow through on his threat to raise tariffs on cars and trucks from the European Union to 25%, the bloc’s finance ministers said.

item.title: Dudley Backs Powell Staying On After ‘Merciless’ Attacks on Fed
item.description: Jerome Powell’s decision to stay on as a Federal Reserve governor after his term as chair will help reassure Wall Street and the public after President Donald Trump’s pressure campaign to lower rates, according to Bill Dudley.

item.title: US Allies Look to Europe to Build a Post-Trump Global Order
item.description: Some of the US’s closest allies including Canada and the UK are warning that the international system of security and trade championed by western powers for more than 70 years is broken beyond repair — and that it’s up to Europe to fashion a new global order.

item.title: Trump-Linked World Liberty Sues Justin Sun for Defamation
item.description: World Liberty Financial said it is suing Justin Sun for defamation, accusing the billionaire crypto entrepreneur of running a coordinated media campaign against the Trump-linked project after he filed his own lawsuit against it last month.

item.title: Goldman, Blackstone Partner With Anthropic on AI Services Firm
item.description: Anthropic PBC is partnering with Blackstone Inc., Hellman & Friedman and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to form a new company that will bring artificial intelligence to a broad group of midsize companies.

item.title: South African Farms Face El Niño Drought Risk on Top of Iran War
item.description: South African farmers pinched by mounting costs from the Iran war now face the added risk of an El Niño-induced drought, with implications for lower agricultural output and higher food prices.

item.title: Standard Chartered to Sell East African Headquarters as Footprint Shrinks
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc is selling its East African head office as the lender shrinks its physical presence on the continent.

item.title: Carlyle Seeds Buyout Fund With $5 Billion in Novel Credit Deal
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. arranged a first-of-its-kind financing that will commit more than $5 billion to seed its next flagship buyout fund while repaying investors in some of its older vintages, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Chile Economic Activity Sees Monthly Rise in End to Weak Quarter
item.description: Chile’s economic activity posted the first monthly gain this year in March, according to a report published days after central bankers warned of a more adverse impact from the Middle East war on both global growth and inflation.

item.title: Banks Kick Off Sale of Junk Bonds Backing BASF Coatings Deal
item.description: Banks have kicked off the sale of roughly €1.95 billion-equivalent ($2.28 billion) of junk bonds to help finance BASF SE’s auto paint and finishing division acquisition by Carlyle Group Inc. and partner Qatar Investment Authority.

item.title: Updates From Milken Institute Global Conference
item.description: Attendees wait for a presentation during the Milken Institute Global Conference in Beverly Hills. Photographer: Patrick T. Fallon/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: Treasuries Start Week Under Pressure as Oil Prices Resume Climb
item.description: Treasuries fell as heightened tensions in the Middle East kept oil prices elevated, fanning concerns over the inflation outlook.

item.title: ADB Urges Countries to Limit Fuel Subsidies to Vulnerable Groups
item.description: Japan and other economies should limit fuel subsidies to those most in need, the Asian Development Bank’s chief economist said, as prolonged Middle East tensions strain public finances.

item.title: OpenAI Finalizes $10 Billion Joint Venture With PE Firms to Deploy AI
item.description: OpenAI has raised more than $4 billion for a new joint venture that will focus on helping businesses adopt its artificial intelligence software, according to a person familiar with the matter.
